display.purpose: Redaction (--font-display) — the ONE display face, used SPARINGLY on login + empty-states ONLY; never tables/labels/buttons/dense UI. The text-display hero-metric SCALE itself rides the body face (Hanken Grotesk) + mono tabular numerics.

headline.purpose: Page titles, rendered through the PageHeader primitive — on the body face (Hanken Grotesk).

body.purpose: The workhorse — table cells, form fields, copy. Prose at 65-75ch; dense tables may run wider.

label.purpose: Uppercase eyebrows and nav section headings (text-label), used sparingly.

mono.purpose: Commit Mono for identifiers, serials, timestamps, audit lines and tabular numerics (tabular-nums).

shadows.name: e1
shadows.purpose: Resting cards, panels, the DetailPanel. The default surface elevation. Dark mode adds an inset top-highlight.

shadows.name: e2
shadows.purpose: Hover/focus on interactive cards — the lift target.

shadows.name: e1-dark
shadows.purpose: Dark-mode e1 — same shape at ~2x alpha plus a faint neutral top-highlight so raised surfaces catch light on the carbon canvas.

motion.name: ease-out-quad
motion.purpose: The workhorse easing — settles, never bounces. Default for transitions and entrances.

motion.name: ease-spring
motion.purpose: Overshoots once. RESERVED for the success checkmark only — do not borrow for general motion.

motion.name: dur-slow
motion.purpose: Entrances (rise-in / fade-in), the cross-route settle, and the check-draw.

motion.name: rise-in
motion.value: @keyframes rise-in { from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(12px); } to { opacity: 1; transform: none; } }
motion.purpose: Component-level surface entrance — 12px rise + fade. Use on cards/rows; never at the route root (transform traps sticky descendants).

motion.name: fade-in
motion.purpose: Opacity-only route-level settle — no transform, so it never establishes a containing block for sticky descendants. app/(app)/template.tsx wraps each route in this.

motion.name: pulse-soft
motion.purpose: The ONE calm attention heartbeat (2.4s) — danger dots only.

motion.name: shimmer
motion.purpose: Skeleton sweep, composed onto the muted skeleton fill at call sites (never edit ui/skeleton.tsx).

motion.name: check-draw
motion.purpose: Success-check stroke draw — the single ease-spring use.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single primary action per view — oxblood stamp fill, used under the One Voice Rule.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; background: var(--primary, oklch(0.52 0.2 25)); color: var(--primary-foreground, oklch(0.985 0 0)); font-family: "Hanken Grotesk", ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.25rem; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; border: none; border-radius: 0.5rem; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px oklch(0.21 0 0 / 0.06); cursor: pointer; transition: background 120ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94), filter 120ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { filter: brightness(1.06); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--ring, oklch(0.52 0.2 25)); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { filter: brightness(0.96); }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: A quiet, neutral-fill action for everything that isn't the one primary.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; background: var(--muted, oklch(0.945 0 0)); color: var(--foreground, oklch(0.18 0 0)); font-family: "Hanken Grotesk", ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.25rem; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; border: 1px solid var(--border, oklch(0.88 0 0)); border-radius: 0.5rem; cursor: pointer; transition: background 120ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: oklch(0.93 0 0); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--ring, oklch(0.52 0.2 25)); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Status Chip
components.description: A solid-fill semantic status pill (StatusBadge) — readable on the paper because it fills solid, never tints text. Includes a leading dot so color is not the sole carrier of meaning.
components.css: .ds-status-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.375rem; background: var(--success, oklch(0.53 0.14 150)); color: var(--success-foreground, oklch(0.985 0 0)); font-family: "Hanken Grotesk", ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1rem; letter-spacing: 0.01em; padding: 0.125rem 0.5rem; border-radius: 0.375rem; } .ds-status-dot { width: 0.375rem; height: 0.375rem; border-radius: 9999px; background: currentColor; }

components.name: Card
components.description: An entry on the page — the recessed 'tape' read by its hairline rule, resting at e1, lifting to e2 on hover via the coordinated lift triad. No nested cards.
components.html:
MacBook Pro 14"

Assigned to Dana Ruiz · Floor 3
components.css: .ds-card { background: var(--card, oklch(0.965 0 0)); color: var(--card-foreground, oklch(0.18 0 0)); border: 1px solid var(--border, oklch(0.88 0 0)); border-radius: 0.875rem; padding: 1.5rem; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px oklch(0.21 0 0 / 0.06), 0 1px 1px oklch(0.21 0 0 / 0.04); transition: transform 180ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94), box-shadow 180ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94); } .ds-card:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: 0 4px 12px -2px oklch(0.21 0 0 / 0.1), 0 2px 4px -2px oklch(0.21 0 0 / 0.06); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-card { transition: box-shadow 0.01ms; } .ds-card:hover { transform: none; } } .ds-card-title { font-family: "Hanken Grotesk", ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1.125rem; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.5rem; letter-spacing: -0.01em; margin: 0 0 0.25rem; } .ds-card-body { font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.25rem; color: var(--muted-foreground, oklch(0.45 0 0)); margin: 0; }

components.name: Search Input
components.description: A calm field with an inline search glyph that comes alive on focus with the oxblood ring — no glow, no glassmorphism.
components.css: .ds-field { position: relative; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; width: 100%; max-width: 20rem; } .ds-field-icon { position: absolute; left: 0.625rem; width: 1rem; height: 1rem; color: var(--muted-foreground, oklch(0.45 0 0)); pointer-events: none; } .ds-input { width: 100%; background: var(--card, oklch(0.965 0 0)); color: var(--foreground, oklch(0.18 0 0)); font-family: "Hanken Grotesk", ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.25rem; padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem 0.5rem 2rem; border: 1px solid var(--border, oklch(0.88 0 0)); border-radius: 0.5rem; outline: none; transition: border-color 120ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94), box-shadow 120ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: var(--muted-foreground, oklch(0.45 0 0)); } .ds-input:focus-visible { border-color: var(--ring, oklch(0.52 0.2 25)); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.52 0.2 25 / 0.25); }

components.name: Sidebar Nav Item (active)
components.description: A sidebar entry in its active state — a muted tint plus the oxblood icon and a 2px state rule, so wayfinding is quiet and never carried by color alone.
components.html: Assets
components.css: .ds-nav-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.625rem; font-family: "Hanken Grotesk", ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.25rem; color: var(--foreground, oklch(0.18 0 0)); padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem; border-radius: 0.5rem; text-decoration: none; transition: background 120ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94); } .ds-nav-icon { width: 1.125rem; height: 1.125rem; flex: none; color: var(--muted-foreground, oklch(0.45 0 0)); } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: var(--sidebar-accent, oklch(0.93 0 0)); } .ds-nav-item--active { background: var(--muted, oklch(0.945 0 0)); color: var(--foreground, oklch(0.18 0 0)); font-weight: 600; box-shadow: inset 2px 0 0 var(--primary, oklch(0.52 0.2 25)); } .ds-nav-item--active .ds-nav-icon { color: var(--primary, oklch(0.52 0.2 25)); } .ds-nav-item:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--ring, oklch(0.52 0.2 25)); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Empty State
components.description: The 'nothing here yet' surface — a pillar-tinted icon chip plus a one-line invitation and an action. One of the two sparse moments where Redaction (the display face) is allowed. Teaches the interface instead of saying 'nothing here'.
components.html:
Nothing here yet

Add your first asset and it shows up here.
components.css: .ds-empty { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; text-align: center; gap: 0.5rem; padding: 2.5rem 1.5rem; animation: ds-rise-in 220ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94) both; } @keyframes ds-rise-in { from { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(12px); } to { opacity: 1; transform: none; } } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-empty { animation-duration: 0.01ms; } } .ds-empty-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 3rem; height: 3rem; border-radius: 9999px; margin-bottom: 0.25rem; background: oklch(0.68 0.11 200 / 0.1); color: var(--pillar-inventory, oklch(0.68 0.11 200)); } .ds-empty-chip svg { width: 1.5rem; height: 1.5rem; } .ds-empty-title { font-family: "Hanken Grotesk", ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1.125rem; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.5rem; letter-spacing: -0.01em; color: var(--foreground, oklch(0.18 0 0)); margin: 0; } .ds-empty-body { font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.25rem; color: var(--muted-foreground, oklch(0.45 0 0)); margin: 0 0 0.5rem; } .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; background: var(--primary, oklch(0.52 0.2 25)); color: var(--primary-foreground, oklch(0.985 0 0)); font-family: "Hanken Grotesk", ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.25rem; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; border: none; border-radius: 0.5rem; cursor: pointer; transition: filter 120ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.46, 0.45, 0.94); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { filter: brightness(1.06); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--ring, oklch(0.52 0.2 25)); outline-offset: 2px; }

narrative.overview: lazyit is a system of record kept by an operator who trusts the page. The canvas is pure paper white — chroma-zero, near-white but never #fff — and the surfaces on it are entries you can act on: cards are the recessed 'tape' just below the paper, read by a hairline rule and lifting when you reach for them under neutral ink shadows. A single disciplined oxblood stamp marks the one action you're taking — a registration mark, not a wall. Color is the labelled column (blue for Access, amber for Knowledge, violet for Manage, cyan for Inventory), never wallpaper, and a cooler security-green seal is reserved for the zero-knowledge verify mark. Data reads in Commit Mono with tabular numerics so identifiers, timestamps and ledger lines are instrument-grade. The system is calm because the operator is mid-task and the record, not the chrome, deserves their attention — but it is alive: things move, settle, and layer with intention. This is the difference between a cared-for ledger and an austere SaaS. Density is earned, not feared. This is the product register: when an operator needs a table of 200 assets or a panel of forty labels, the system gives it to them densely and legibly — tabular numerics, a tight type scale, consistent affordances screen to screen. The energy never comes from louder buttons or more CTAs; it comes from motion, depth, and color identity layered onto an otherwise quiet surface. Restraint is the floor; craft is how we activate it. The Ledger explicitly rejects the things that make internal tools feel un-cared-for: the generic AI-slop dashboard, the AI-cream tell, heavy/dated ServiceNow, SaaS landing-page clichés, glassmorphism, gradient text, tiny uppercase eyebrows on every section, the hollow hero-metric template, and austere SaaS coldness. It does not chase delight on every page — delight is saved for moments (a success checkmark, an empty state that invites), never sprayed across the surface.
keyCharacteristics: Pure-neutral paper/carbon canvas, chroma 0 — near-white paper, near-black carbon, never #fff/#000 or the warm 'bone'/AI-cream tell (ADR-0077).
One disciplined oxblood stamp for action, selection, and state — ≤10% of any screen; a registration mark, not a wall.
Five categorical/pillar hues DECOUPLED from the brand — tint/chip/dot/border only, never readable text (Access→blue, Knowledge→amber, Manage→violet, Inventory→cyan, + a magenta 5th series).
A security-green verify seal (--verify) reserved for the zero-knowledge / Secret-Manager mark, distinct from the everyday emerald --success.
A neutral, named elevation scale — chroma-0 ink shadows; objects rest at e1 and lift on interaction.
A single CSS motion vocabulary — 120–220ms, state-conveying, reduced-motion-safe, zero JS lib.
A type trio — Hanken Grotesk (body/UI/headings) + Commit Mono (data/IDs/numerics) + Redaction (display, login + empty-states only) — on a fixed rem scale.
WCAG-AA by construction in both themes; color never carries meaning alone.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: The oxblood stamp is reserved for action, selection, and state — primary buttons, focus rings, the active nav item — and used on ≤10% of any given screen. Its rarity is the point: it is a registration mark, not a wall. Oxblood is never decoration, never a section background, never a 'let's add some color here' flourish.

rules.name: The Pillar-as-Decoration Rule
rules.body: A pillar or chart hue may be a tint, a border, a dot, or a chip background — never readable text on the paper or on a /10 tint. The categorical ramp is DECOUPLED from the brand (oxblood ~25) and from verify (green ~162): Access→blue, Knowledge→amber, Manage→violet, Inventory(+Consumables)→cyan, plus a magenta 5th series. Each pillar aliases a --chart-* hue (which .dark redefines lighter), so it cannot clear 4.5:1 as body text. Decorative glyphs ≥24px inside a bg-pillar-*/10 chip are exempt; everything readable stays on --foreground / --card-foreground or a semantic *-foreground.

rules.name: The Verify-Seal Rule
rules.body: The security-green --verify seal is RESERVED for the Secret Manager / zero-knowledge mark — the cryptographic 'VERIFIED' seal — and is kept DISTINCT from the everyday emerald --success (operational/good), so 'operational' never reads as 'cryptographically verified'. Like every semantic token it fills solid with its AA-cleared --verify-foreground; it is never a decorative green.

rules.name: The Body-Carries-Everything Rule
rules.body: The body face — Hanken Grotesk, with Commit Mono for data/IDs/numerics — carries the entire UI: headings, buttons, labels, body, tables, dense data. Redaction is the ONE display face, used SPARINGLY on login + empty-states ONLY — never in a label, button, data cell, or any product-register surface. The rare display face is the exception that proves the discipline, not a free-for-all; the IT-native voice holds.

rules.name: The Paper-and-Ink Rule
rules.body: Shadows are neutral chroma-0 ink (oklch 0.21 0 0), never warm-tinted or clinical gray — paper on a desk under even light. Surfaces rest at e1 and lift on interaction. The signature is the lift recipe: the coordinated hover triad -translate-y-0.5 + e1→e2 + ring /10→/15, fired together, so a card hover feels crafted, not a flat background swap. In dark the same shapes run at ~2x alpha plus a faint neutral inset top-highlight (oklch 0.97 0 0 / 0.04) so raised surfaces catch light on the carbon canvas. Never apply lift to static table rows.

dos: Do keep the oxblood stamp to ≤10% of a screen — action, selection, and state only (The One Voice Rule).
Do express pillar identity via a tinted icon chip (bg-pillar-*/10 + an aria-hidden glyph), a dot, or a subtle background tint — on the brand-decoupled categorical ramp.
Do rest surfaces at shadow-e1 and lift on interaction with the lift recipe (-translate-y-0.5 + e1→e2 + ring /10→/15).
Do reserve the security-green --verify seal for the zero-knowledge / Secret-Manager mark, kept distinct from the everyday --success.
Do keep all motion in CSS + tw-animate-css, 150–250ms, conveying state, and reduced-motion-safe.
Do treat the pure-neutral paper/carbon canvas as a deliberate, committed brand decision (ADR-0077) — chroma 0, never the warm bone or the AI-cream tell.
Do give metric cards real substance — deep-linked breakdowns, tabular numerics in Commit Mono — not a hollow number.
Do keep readable text on --foreground / --card-foreground, or use a semantic StatusBadge solid-fill with its AA-verified *-foreground.
Do ship the full state set on every interactive component (default, hover, focus, active, disabled, loading) and the same vocabulary screen to screen.
donts: Don't lean on SaaS landing clichés.
Don't put tiny uppercase eyebrows on every section.
Don't ship the hollow hero-metric template.

Don't use border-left / border-right greater than 1px as a colored pillar accent on cards, rows, callouts, or alerts (an absolute ban). The narrow exception: a ≤2px active-nav selection rule is allowed as a state indicator — but prefer bg-tint + weight + icon color.

Don't add a JS motion library (framer-motion, gsap, anime, lenis). No orchestrated page-load choreography: the route settle is a subtle opacity fade, and the metric stagger is minimal and initial-mount only.
Don't reintroduce a warm or cream tint to the neutral canvas — the chroma-0 paper is a committed decision (ADR-0077); warmth now reads as the AI-cream tell it replaced.
Don't use a pillar/chart hue as readable text on the paper or on a /10 tint. Keep text on --foreground / --card-foreground or a semantic StatusBadge solid-fill.
Don't put Redaction in product-register UI — labels, buttons, data cells, dense tables; the display face is login + empty-states only.
